t_ch: 将军所言极是。此外，探子也打探到了不少情报，有劳花火将军详述一二。
t_en: The General speaks wisely. Furthermore, our scouts have gathered quite a bit of intelligence. General Sparkle, would you kindly elaborate on the details?

t_ch: 花火什么？
t_en: Wait, General Sparkle?

t_ch: 自导自演是吧？
t_en: So you're just casting yourself now?

t_ch: 怎么，你当得将军，我当不得？
t_en: What, you're allowed to be a general and I'm not?

t_ch: 咳…据刺探来的情报，这位绝灭大君可不简单哪。传闻里它生有獠牙四足，通体雪白，不出世则已，一出世便灾异横行，生灵涂炭。
t_en: Ahem... According to our sources, this Lord Ravager isn't your everyday monster. Rumor has it that it has four legs, fangs, and snow-white hide. It only appears in times of great chaos, bringing disaster wherever it goes.

t_ch: 就没点有用的信息吗？
t_en: Any chance we could get some useful intel?

t_ch: 哎，将军有所不知。细节决定成败，保不准小道消息里，就藏着制敌之道呢~
t_en: Come now, General. Victory lies in the details. Sometimes, it's those little rumors that hold the key to winning the war~

t_ch: 你说的这位大君，它是人吗？
t_en: This Overlord you're talking about... Is it even human?

t_ch: 哎，只要破坏力够强，是不是人形又有什么关系呢？
t_en: As long as it's destructive enough, who cares if it's human or not?

t_ch: 无妨。有诸位将军坐镇，仙舟上下一心、同仇敌忾，定能将那绝灭大君彻底剿灭！
t_en: Fair enough. With all of you holding the line, and the whole Xianzhou standing united, we will bring down that Lord Ravager!

t_ch: 哈哈，当将军真威风啊。
t_en: Haha, being a general feels so amazing.

t_ch: 虽远，必诛！
t_en: Far or near, we shall strike them down!

t_ch: 前线来报，敌军主力被尽数歼灭。但大敌仍作困兽之斗，率领残党直奔此处来了。
t_en: Urgent report! The enemy's main force has been obliterated. But the Overlord's not done yet. It's leading a desperate charge straight toward us.

t_ch: 跟我的球棒去说吧！
t_en: Let it come. My bat's got a few words for it.

t_ch: 必叫它有来无回！
t_en: It's not leaving this place alive!

t_ch: 佩佩大君，驾到汪！
t_en: Behold! The mighty Overlord Peppy has arrived! Woof!

t_ch: 在这里的所有人，统统发配到「禁闭舱段」擦地板，一个不留！
t_en: Everyone here, straight to the seclusion zone. Grab a mop and start scrubbing. No exceptions!

t_ch: …佩佩会说话了？
t_en: ...Peppy can talk now?

t_ch: 你怎么成了绝灭大君？！
t_en: How did you become the Lord Ravager!?

t_ch: 汪汪！没想到吧，因为这里是匹诺康尼——
t_en: Woof woof! Bet you didn't see that coming. This is Penacony!

t_ch: 每个人都能实现自己的梦想汪！
t_en: Here, anyone's dreams can come true, woof!

t_ch: 我打听到了它的出生年月、星座血型、饮食忌口，将军想听哪一个？
t_en: I even found its birthday, zodiac sign, blood type, and food allergies. Which one do you want, General?

t_ch: 你清醒过来，发现自己趴在桌子上睡着了。
t_en: You wake up, slumped over the table. Turns out you'd dozed off.

t_ch: 你醒啦，小灰毛？俗话说日有所思，夜有所梦。原来你每天都想着这些事呀。
t_en: Rise and shine, Gray Hair. Like they say, think about something all day, and you'll dream about it at night. So this is what's been on your mind, huh?

t_ch: 好险，有些失态了。
t_en: Whew, that was close. Got a little carried away there.

t_ch: 我应该没说梦话吧？
t_en: I didn't sleep talk, did I?

t_ch: 放心放心，花火将军什么都没听到哦~
t_en: Don't worry. General Sparkle didn't hear a thing~

t_ch: 我的这杯大作如何？好好想想，什么样的名字才能配得上它呢？
t_en: So? What do you think of my latest masterpiece? Come on, give it a name worthy of its greatness!

t_ch: 「银河梦幻共演」。
t_en: "Galactic Dream Duet."

t_ch: 这个阵容太豪华了，是不花信用点能看的吗？
t_en: This lineup is insane! Are you sure we can watch this without using any credits?

t_ch: 「人生体验卡」。
t_en: "Life Experience Card."

t_ch: 戒断反应好严重，有时长是永久的体验卡就好了。
t_en: These withdrawal symptoms are brutal... If only it came with unlimited playtime.

t_ch: 你喝下调饮，突然间眼前一黑。
t_en: You take a sip... and everything goes black.

t_ch: 不是，给我读到哪个档来了？
t_en: Hold up, what save file did I just load into?

t_ch: 吧台失去了调饮师，如同神策府没有将军坐镇，可是天大的事。赶紧回去看看吧。
t_en: The bar's lost its drinksmith, just like the Seat of Divine Foresight without a general. That's a major issue. You'd better get back there, stat.

t_ch: 呵呵，年轻人真是精力充沛啊。
t_en: Heh, youth sure is something else.

t_ch: 临行前竟然还抽空兼职，是零花钱不够用了么？需不需要我替你找帕姆预支些？
t_en: Even squeezing in a side gig before heading out? Outta pocket money, are we? Want me to ask Pom-Pom for an advance?

t_ch: 我是一个兴趣使然的调饮师，不在乎这些身外之物。
t_en: I mix drinks purely for the thrill of it. Worldly possessions mean nothing to me.

t_ch: 我唯一的使命，就是调饮！姬子，杨叔，要不要试试我的手艺？
t_en: My one true calling is mixology! Himeko, Mr. Yang, care for a taste?

t_ch: 千万不能喝！
t_en: Don't drink that!

t_ch: 不许说我的台词——
t_en: Hey! That's my line!

t_ch: 你气喘吁吁地向二人说明了原委。
t_en: Gasping for breath, you quickly fill them in on what just happened.

t_ch: 你说我是花火假扮的，我还说你是异星水魈变的呢。
t_en: You say I'm just Sparkle in disguise? Please. For all I know, you're a Xenohydro in cosplay.

t_ch: 嗯…愚者改头换面的本领的确了得，光凭外表，的确难以分辨。
t_en: Hmm... Gotta admit, those shapeshifting tricks are impressive. Can't judge by looks alone.

t_ch: 不如你们比比看，谁更像真正的{NICKNAME}吧？
t_en: How about a challenge? Let's see who pulls off a better impression of {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 求之不得！输的一方，干脆人道毁灭好了吧。
t_en: Gladly! Loser gets vaporized. Nice and simple.

t_ch: 身正不怕影子斜。
t_en: A clear conscience fears no crooked shadow.

t_ch: 花火这招太狠了…
t_en: That was a brutal move, Sparkle...

t_ch: 第一回合。请听题，当在路边偶遇垃圾桶，真正的{NICKNAME}会怎么做？
t_en: Round one. Here's your question: You're walking down the street and come across a trashcan. What would the real {NICKNAME} do?

t_ch: 那还用说，当然是掏一掏咯。
t_en: Isn't it obvious? Gotta dig in and see what's inside.

t_ch: 我会贴上去。
t_en: I'd stick myself onto it.

t_ch: 垃圾桶壁冰冰凉凉的，摸起来很舒服。
t_en: The walls of a trashcan can are cool to the touch — feels kinda nice, actually.

t_ch: 垃圾桶早过气了！
t_en: Trashcans are old news!

t_ch: 我们注定只能陪伴彼此走过人生的一小段旅程。现在，该向前看了。
t_en: We're destined to only accompany each other for a small part of life's journey. Now, it's time to look ahead.

t_ch: 听起来都是{NICKNAME}的作风…各计一分吧。
t_en: Sounds like something {NICKNAME} would say... one point to each of you.

t_ch: 第二回合。既然在酒吧，就问个有关调饮的问题吧，真正的{NICKNAME}最爱的饮品是……
t_en: Round two. Since we're in a bar, let's talk drinks. What's {NICKNAME}'s go-to beverage?

t_ch: 仙人快乐茶。
t_en: Immortal's Delight.

t_ch: 我不是仙人，但我从中也得到了快乐。
t_en: I'm no immortal, but that tea brings me joy.

t_ch: 三月七经常找我凑单，又是一个小细节。这下总该信我了吧！
t_en: March 7th always drags me into splitting orders with her. Just another detail proving I'm the real deal!

t_ch: 苏乐达。
t_en: SoulGlad.

t_ch: 在匹诺康尼怎么能少了苏乐达呢。经典永不过时。
t_en: How could you not mention SoulGlad in Penacony? A timeless classic.

t_ch: 最近在控糖…
t_en: I'm cutting back on sugar recently...

t_ch: 我立志不喝饮料，目前已经坚持了整整半个系统时。
t_en: I swore off drinks. Haven't had one for half a system hour.
Mmm...

t_ch: 这个问题还用思考吗？当然是姬子姐姐的咖啡！
t_en: Do we even have to ask? Of course it's Himeko's coffee!

t_ch: 回答正确。你得一分。
t_en: Correct answer. You get one point.

t_ch: 有黑幕？
t_en: Rigged!

t_ch: 我也喜欢，我也喜欢的！
t_en: I like it too! I really do!

t_ch: 晚了！你喜欢并不是发自真心。
t_en: Too late! You're just saying that to win.

t_ch: 那么，比赛进行第三回合…真是胶着啊。真正的{NICKNAME}身手不凡，各自露一手给我们瞧瞧吧。
t_en: Now for round three... it's a tight match. Time to show off your skills — let's see what {NICKNAME} can really do!

t_ch: 终于到真刀真枪的环节了吗？
t_en: Finally, time for some real action!

t_ch: 不战斗就无法生存！
t_en: You fight to survive!

t_ch: 你掏了掏背包…但本应放着球棒的地方空空如也。
t_en: You reach into your bag... but the spot where your bat should be is unoccupied.

t_ch: 你在找的，不会是这个吧？
t_en: Looking for this?

t_ch: 规则，就是用来打破的！
t_en: Rules are made to be broken!

t_ch: 综合看来…我想谁是真正的{NICKNAME}，答案已经很明显了。
t_en: Well, taking everything into account... it's pretty clear who the real {NICKNAME} is.

t_ch: 谢谢各位评委，谢谢到场的嘉宾朋友和支持我的每一个人。也由衷地感谢惊梦酒吧，感谢酒馆，感谢星穹列车——
t_en: Thank you, judges. Thank you to our wonderful audience, and to everyone who believed in me. Special thanks to Dreamjolt Hostelry, to this fine establishment, and to the Astral Express—

t_ch: 胜负已经分明，你就不必再装了吧，花火小姐？
t_en: The game's over. No need to keep pretending, Sparkle.

t_ch: 其实我才是真的…
t_en: But... I am the real one...

t_ch: 真正的死亡，是被人遗忘……
t_en: Real death... is being forgotten...

t_ch: 难道我真是花火？
t_en: Wait. Am I actually Sparkle?

t_ch: 如果我是花火，也好。
t_en: If I were Sparkle... that wouldn't be so bad.

t_ch: 谢谢大家，当然也谢谢我可敬的对手，我最好的榜样。
t_en: Thanks, everyone. And a special thanks to my worthy rival — my greatest inspiration.

t_ch: 请允许我郑重宣布，在模仿{NICKNAME}大赛上夺得冠军的是——
t_en: Now, allow me to announce the winner of the {NICKNAME} Impersonator Contest—

t_ch: 这一位。
t_en: This one.

t_ch: 杨叔，你真是糊涂了，我站在这儿呢！
t_en: Mr. Yang, are you blind? I'm right here!

t_ch: 很遗憾，花火小姐，我没有选错。真正的{NICKNAME}，是绝不会说出「喜欢姬子的咖啡」这种话的。
t_en: I'm afraid not, Miss Sparkle. I chose correctly. The real {NICKNAME} would never say something like "I like Himeko's coffee."

t_ch: …尽管我并不想承认这点。别太灰心，第二名付出的努力也应当受到奖励。
t_en: ...Much as I hate to admit it. Don't be too discouraged, second place deserves a reward too.

t_ch: 还有这种好事，奖品是什么？
t_en: Now that's what I like to hear. What's the prize?

t_ch: 一杯你最爱的手调咖啡。
t_en: A fresh cup of your favorite hand-crafted coffee.

t_ch: 对不起，请容我拒绝！
t_en: Sorry, I must decline.

t_ch: 杨叔和姬子点了两杯调饮，主宾尽欢后，两人向你告别离去。
t_en: Mr. Yang and Himeko each ordered a drink. After a good laugh and some cheerful banter, they bid you farewell.

t_ch: 不，这是两个热爱咖啡的灵魂之间的共鸣！
t_en: No, this is a resonance between two coffee-loving souls!

t_ch: 还挺好玩的吧。小灰毛，这杯调饮是不是特别地超出想象？
t_en: Pretty fun, huh? So, Gray Hair, wasn't that drink just... wildly unexpected?

t_ch: 效果太简单直接了吧。
t_en: The effect is a little too direct, if you ask me.

t_ch: 原来是字面上的睡梦…
t_en: So the "dream" part was... literal?

t_ch: 可别小瞧它，没准是喝了后，就会「嗖」地传送到另一处的神奇调饮也说不定呢~
t_en: Don't underestimate it. Who knows, maybe it'll whisk you straight to another place like some kind of teleporting drink!

t_ch: 好啦，别在意这种细节，给它起个一听就非同凡响的名字吧？
t_en: Anyway, don't sweat the details and help me name it! Something catchy and out of this world!

t_ch: 「昏睡铁道」。
t_en: Sleep Rail.

t_ch: 这地方越来越像黑店了…配方再试下去，真担心猎犬查上门啊。
t_en: This place is starting to feel like a shady bar... If we keep testing recipes, the Bloodhounds will be knocking on our door.

t_ch: 「梦里全都有」。
t_en: All-Containing Dream.

t_ch: 年轻就是好，可以倒头就睡。
t_en: Youth really is a gift. You can fall asleep anywhere, anytime.

t_ch: 哇哦，这位顾客，你的运气可真好！
t_en: Whoa! Dear customer, lucky you!

t_ch: 你是花火大人今天接待的*第一位*顾客，获得了「再来一杯」奖励！现在就兑奖吧？
t_en: You're Sparkle's first customer of the day — congrats! That means you win a bonus round: "One More Cup"! Ready to redeem?

t_ch: 不喝白不喝。
t_en: Free drinks? Count me in.

t_ch: 感谢支持！我一定给你多加些料~
t_en: Thanks for the support! I'll throw in something extra special~

t_ch: 不好意思，婉拒了。
t_en: Sorry, I'll pass.

t_ch: 真扫兴，不是谁都有机会喝到花火大人的特调哦~
t_en: Boo, you're no fun! Not everyone gets to taste a Sparkle original~

t_ch: 好啦，你的宝贝「特调配方」还给你。我把试过的配方都记下了，不要太感谢我哦。
t_en: Alright, here's your precious "custom recipe" back. I wrote down all the combos we tried. You're welcome!

t_ch: 记住了，酒馆可是愚者的地盘，下回有什么好玩的，一定要先通知我！
t_en: Just remember: This tavern belongs to the Fools. If anything fun's going on, I better be the first to know!

t_ch: （翻翻配方）
t_en: (You flip through the recipe...)

t_ch: …居然还写得有模有样的。在意想不到的时候认真，也是一种愚弄吗？
t_en: Huh. Surprisingly detailed. Maybe taking things seriously at random moments is her way of fooling people too?

t_ch: 这个配方就试到这里。是时候去复原最后一份「特调配方」了！
t_en: That's all I have for recipe-testing. Time to recreate the final "Special Mix Recipe"!

t_ch: 最后一份配方字迹模糊，已经几近空白，真的有可能复原吗？
t_en: The last recipe is so faded it's almost blank. Can it really be restored?

t_ch: 不管那么多，先开张吧！
t_en: No point worrying about it. Let's open for business!

t_ch: 今天会遇见什么样的客人呢……
t_en: I wonder who'll walk through that door today...

t_ch: 酒吧内静悄悄的，想象中的顾客并没有出现。
t_en: The lounge is quiet. The expected guests never show up.

t_ch: 派对总有结束的时候。
t_en: Every party comes to an end.

t_ch: 人生起起落落，不能期待每一刻都像节日那般精彩。
t_en: Life rises and falls. You can't expect every moment to feel like a celebration.

t_ch: 这就是现实。
t_en: That's just reality.

t_ch: 公司和家族不对付，经济不景气，大环境不好…总之，生意越来难做了。
t_en: The IPC and The Family are at odds, the economy's down, the broader environment is tough... all in all, business is harder than ever.

t_ch: （再等一会儿吧。）
t_en: (Let's wait a little longer.)

t_ch: 不知过去了多久……
t_en: No one knows how much time has passed...

t_ch: 你有些想打哈欠了。
t_en: You're starting to feel a yawn coming on.

t_ch: 这份工作太轻松了。
t_en: This job is far too easy.

t_ch: 你怀着成为一名调饮大师的志向背井离乡来到这里，却一整天游手好闲、无所事事地站在柜台后…难道你满意吗？
t_en: You left home dreaming of becoming a great drinksmith, yet here you are, spending your days idle behind the counter... Is this really what you wanted?

t_ch: 答案当然是——比起「开拓」，你更喜欢这样的生活！
t_en: The answer is clear. Compared to "Trailblazing," you enjoy this kind of life more!

t_ch: 难不成酒吧已经过气了？
t_en: Has the lounge already gone out of style?

t_ch: 时尚潮流的更迭迅速而残酷，你不禁开始同情起匹诺康尼所有的调饮师来。
t_en: Fashion trends shift with brutal speed, and you can't help but feel a twinge of sympathy for every drinksmith in Penacony.

t_ch: （再等一会儿吧。）
t_en: (Let's wait a little longer.)

t_ch: 不知过去了多久……
t_en: No one knows how much time has passed...

t_ch: 一阵困意袭来。
t_en: Sleepiness creeps in.

t_ch: 眯一会儿，就一小会儿。
t_en: Just a nap. Just for a moment.

t_ch: 不可以，马上就能领完奖励了，绝对不可以在这里结束！
t_en: No, you're so close to claiming your reward. Absolutely don't let it end here.

t_ch: 绝对…不可以……
t_en: Absolutely... mustn't...

t_ch: 对…可以……
t_en: End... here...

t_ch: 绝对不能在岗位上倒下！
t_en: Do not collapse on duty!

t_ch: 尽管哈欠连天，但你是一个有职业素养的调饮师，会坚守岗位到最后一秒。
t_en: Even though you're yawning nonstop, you're a professional drinksmith. You'll stay at your post until the very last second.

t_ch: 最后…一秒…这是你意识清晰的最后一秒。糟糕，恐怕不会有人叫你起床。
t_en: The last... second... This is your final moment of clarity. Too bad, there may be no one around to wake you up.

t_ch: 困意淹没了你。
t_en: Sleep overtakes you.

t_ch: 不知多久之后……
t_en: Who knows how long has passed since...

t_ch: 这…是…哪里？
t_en: Where... am I?

t_ch: 阿斯德纳星系的某支边缘旋臂上，忆质包裹的梦中，又或者…旅途的又一站。
t_en: Somewhere on a remote spiral arm of the Asdana system, in a dream shrouded by memoria, or perhaps... just another stop along the journey.

t_ch: 这些答案，你更喜欢哪个？
t_en: Which answer do you prefer?

t_ch: 我…睡了多久？
t_en: I... how long was I asleep?

t_ch: 如我所见的，约莫十五分钟。至于在此之前，答案恐怕只有你自己知道了。
t_en: From when I started counting, about fifteen minutes. As for before that, I'm afraid only you can say.

t_ch: …这是个梦吗？
t_en: ...Is this a dream?

t_ch: 这是梦吗？或许吧。答案只有你自己才能决定。
t_en: A dream? Maybe. Only you can decide.

t_ch: 嘘，不必告诉我，也不必对我的贸然来访感到讶异。投进湖心的石子总会泛起波澜，但余波终归需要有人来抚平。
t_en: Shh, there's no need to tell me, and no need to be surprised by my sudden appearance. A stone thrown into a lake will cause ripples, but those ripples eventually need someone to calm them.

t_ch: 当然…你也不必为此担心。
t_en: Still... it's not something you need to worry about.

t_ch: 你们为什么来这里？
t_en: Why are you here?

t_ch: 你会知晓事情的全貌，但不是在今天。这是眼下我能给你的答案。
t_en: You'll learn the full picture, but not today. That's all I can say for now.

t_ch: 我更担心匹诺康尼…
t_en: I'm more worried about Penacony...

t_ch: 你瞧，匹诺康尼依旧安然无恙。我向你保证，你挂虑的事不会发生。
t_en: See? Penacony is still perfectly fine. I assure you, the thing you're worried about won't happen.

t_ch: 在告别之前，就像寻常的调饮师那样，把我当作偶然路过、且不会再来的过客，为我调一杯特饮，再说一番漫不经心的闲话吧。
t_en: Before we part, just do what any drinksmith would, treat me like a passerby who won't return. Fix me a special drink, and let's share a few idle words.

t_ch: 至于调出什么样的饮品，<color=#dbc291ff>就交给你自己选择。</color>
t_en: As for what kind of drink to prepare, <color=#dbc291ff>that's for you to decide.</color>

t_ch: 该起床了。
t_en: Time to get up.

t_ch: （开始调饮）
t_en: (Mixing begins)

t_ch: 请慢用。
t_en: Please enjoy.

t_ch: （递上调饮。）
t_en: (Hands over the drink.)

t_ch: 谢谢。
t_en: Thank you.

t_ch: （附赠一个冷笑话。）
t_en: (An eye-rolling joke as a bonus.)

t_ch: 提问：星核猎手的藏身处在公司的打击下化为一片焦炭，为什么只有银狼的电脑安然无恙？
t_en: Question: The Stellaron Hunters' hideout was burned to ashes by an IPC strike, so why was Silver Wolf's computer the only thing left intact?

t_ch: 答案：因为她安装了「防火墙」。
t_en: Answer: Because she had a "firewall."

t_ch: 呵呵…很有趣， 我会转述给她听。
t_en: Heh... amusing. I'll relay this to her.

t_ch: 液体沿着玻璃杯壁淌过她的嘴唇。复杂的神情在她的脸上酝酿…姑且可以视作满意？
t_en: The liquid slid along the edge of the glass and touched her lips. A complex look formed on her face... maybe that meant she was satisfied?

t_ch: 意料之外的味道呢…像是极光。
t_en: Unexpected flavor... like the aurora.

t_ch: 绚烂、美妙、捉摸不透。趣味盎然的享受，作为旅程的收尾，再好不过了。
t_en: Brilliant, beautiful, and elusive. A delightful experience, and a perfect way to end the journey.

t_ch: 我之前也去过不少地方，喝过不少饮品，有兴趣听听看吗？
t_en: I've traveled to many places before and tasted many drinks. Want to hear about them?

t_ch: 当然！
t_en: Of course!

t_ch: 请便。
t_en: Go ahead.

t_ch: 有一次，任务结束后，在公司物流舰的首席办公室，我按下了办公桌上的按钮。结果，智械侍者摇摇晃晃地送来了饮品。
t_en: After a mission, I once pressed a button in the executive office of an IPC logistics ship, and a wobbling Intellitron butler came in with a drink.

t_ch: 为什么会去那里？
t_en: Why were you there?

t_ch: 剧本上这么写，我们便这么做了。
t_en: The script said so, so we did it.

t_ch: 说起来奇怪…我已经很少考虑「为什么」。当命运把缘由和结果的间距拉长到匪夷所思的尺度，「为什么」就成了一个苍白无力的问题。
t_en: Funny thing... I've stopped thinking about "why." When destiny stretches the space between cause and effect to something absurd, "why" loses all meaning.

t_ch: 为什么随便乱按？
t_en: Why press a random button?

t_ch: 按常理，那是舰船的自毁按钮。但显然，他们没有按常理出牌。
t_en: Normally, it would be the ship's self-destruct trigger. But clearly, they weren't following the norm.

t_ch: 口味怎么样？
t_en: How did it taste?

t_ch: 那是一杯无糖饮料，味道实在乏善可陈。矫饰欲望再声称它的无害，公司就爱做这种事。
t_en: It was a sugar-free drink, so it's dull and uninspired. The IPC loves to repackage desire and pretend it's harmless, that's their style.

t_ch: 还有一次，在某颗星球的极地，消退的引力搅动冰川的潮汐。我路过那里，顺手从深海翻涌上来的冰川表面凿下一块坚冰，放进了玻璃杯里。
t_en: Another time, I passed through the polar region of a planet where fading gravity churned the tides beneath the glaciers. I chipped off a chunk of ice rising from the deep sea and dropped it into a glass.

t_ch: 引力为什么会消退？
t_en: Why did the gravity recede?

t_ch: 它的伴星碎了。至于原因，你得问萨姆。
t_en: Its companion star shattered. If you want the reason, ask SAM.

t_ch: 不会有远古细菌吗？
t_en: Wouldn't there be ancient bacteria?

t_ch: 呵呵…银狼也问了这个问题。
t_en: Heh... Silver Wolf asked the same thing.

t_ch: 口感会好一些吗？
t_en: Did it taste better?

t_ch: 只记得很凉了。搭配那杯饮料倒是刚好。
t_en: I just remember it was cold. It went well with the drink.

t_ch: 当然还有我们常光顾的那颗星球。它有漫长的海岸线，变动不居的轴倾角让旅客能在一次漫步里体验不同的季节。
t_en: Then there's the planet we used to frequent. Long coastlines, an ever-shifting axial tilt that lets you feel the seasons change as you walk.

t_ch: 说起来有些狼狈，我们凑够身上所有的信用点，才在海边的小摊买到一打橘子汽水。
t_en: It was a bit of a mess, we scraped together all the credits we had just to buy a dozen orange sodas from a beach stall.

t_ch: 星核猎手也会缺钱？
t_en: Even Stellaron Hunters can run out of money?

t_ch: 一些人兴高采烈地出了坏主意，一些人没来得及阻止，另一些人就已经执行完毕…最后，就会碰见这样捉襟见肘的时候。
t_en: Some people gleefully hatched a terrible plan, and some other people went through with it before a third group managed to stop it... That's how we ended up running out of funds.

t_ch: 「我们」…
我们…那时的星核猎手。
t_en: We... The Stellaron Hunters of the time.

t_ch: 味道如何？
t_en: How did it taste?

t_ch: 还能是什么，咸的海风、阳光和橘子的味道。
t_en: What else could it be? The flavor of salty sea breeze, sunlight, and oranges.

t_ch: 唔，不知不觉，剧本段落之间换行的空白就快结束了。
t_en: Mmph. Before you know it, the blank space between the script's segments has almost come to an end.

t_ch: 尽管总觉得时间匆匆，但走过的地方多了，就难免留下些回忆。
t_en: Though time always feels fleeting, the more places you wander, the more memories you gather.

t_ch: 今天的这杯，我也会好好记住。{NICKNAME}，<color=#dbc291ff>听我说——</color>
t_en: I'll remember this one too, {NICKNAME}. <color=#dbc291ff>Listen...</color>

t_ch: 你未曾见过我。但玻璃杯里泛起的涟漪，会和漂泊的星光一样，为我们记下这瞬间。
t_en: You've never seen me, but the ripples in this glass, like the drifting starlight, will remember this moment for both of us.

t_ch: 多谢款待，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Thanks for the drink, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 这为我们增加了不少工作量。不过无关痛痒，最后刃处理了侍者，我处理了饮料。
t_en: It added a bit of work for us, but no harm done. In the end, Blade took care of the bartender, and I handled the drinks.

t_ch: 你醒转过来，发现自己趴在吧台上睡着了。零零散散来了些客人，但都默契地没有打扰你。
t_en: You stir awake, sprawled across the bar counter. A few scattered guests drifted in and out, all in tacit agreement not to disturb you.

t_ch: 怎么又是这样？
t_en: This again?

t_ch: 果然是个梦吗…
t_en: So it really was a dream...

t_ch: 玻璃杯、吧匙、摇饮器，都安然地待在原地。没有证据证明卡芙卡未曾来过，就像没有痕迹证明她的确来过。
t_en: The glass, spoon, and shaker are all in place. Just as there's no proof Kafka never came, there's no trace she ever did.

t_ch: 但是方才调饮的配方依然清晰。你打算把它记录在「特调配方」的最后一页，作为整个宇宙最了解它的人，只有你有资格添上这一笔。
t_en: But the drink recipe remains vividly clear. You decide to record it on the final page of your "Signature Recipes." As the one who understands it better than anyone in the universe, only you are fit to leave this mark.

t_ch: 那么，给这杯调饮起个名字吧！
t_en: Then let's name this drink!

t_ch: 「开拓者特调•Remake」。
t_en: Trailblazer's Special: Remake.

t_ch: 「群星交汇的轨迹」。
t_en: Traces of Stars Converging.

t_ch: 配方试得差不多了。
t_en: Most of the recipes have been tested out.

t_ch: 向舒翁报告吧。
t_en: Go report to Siobhan.

t_ch: 该找舒翁领工钱了！
t_en: Time to collect my pay from Siobhan!

t_ch: 欢迎回来，亲爱的开拓者，调饮辛苦了。您需要来杯饮品、一些小食，还是一些新鲜的幽默？
t_en: Welcome back, dear Trailblazer. You've worked hard. Would you like a drink, some snacks, or a fresh bit of humor?

t_ch: 怎么是你？
t_en: You?

t_ch: 有种宾至如归的体验。
t_en: Feels just like home.

t_ch: 之前周末只有两天，不足以让我下车闲逛，但现在情况好多了。
t_en: Back then, weekends were only two days long, not nearly enough time to get off and explore. Things are much better now.

t_ch: 因为列车上又多了一个「星期日」。
t_en: Because there's an extra "Sunday" aboard the Astral Express.

t_ch: 哦，我需要提醒您注意：这只是一个笑话，星期日先生其实并不能延长周末。虽然他的确尝试过。
t_en: Oh, I should note, that's just a joke. Mr. Sunday doesn't actually extend the weekend, although he certainly tried.

t_ch: 舒翁女士说要替离去的友人照看孩子，因此把酒吧经营暂时托付给了我。而我，亲爱的开拓者，我只是个路过的酒保而已。
t_en: Miss Siobhan said she must care for a departed friend's child, so she entrusted the lounge to me for now. And I, dear Trailblazer, am merely a wandering drinksmith.

t_ch: 她又随机抓路人上班了。
t_en: She's pulled in a random passerby for work again.

t_ch: 「替友人照看孩子」？
t_en: "Looking after a friend's child"?

t_ch: 舒翁女士临行前念念有词，我依稀听见她说「眠眠还留在热砂会场，得接它回家」…恐怕的确是非去不可的要事。
t_en: Before leaving, Miss Siobhan was muttering to herself. I faintly heard her say, "Sleepie is still at the Scorchsand Hall. I need to bring it home." Seems like it was something truly urgent.

t_ch: 哦，对了，亲爱的开拓者，听舒翁女士说，您正忙于钻研「特调配方」，我对此很感兴趣。既然您现在有空闲，或许我有幸能品尝一杯？
t_en: Oh, right, dear Trailblazer, I heard from Miss Siobhan that you've been working on some "Signature Recipes." That piqued my interest. Since you're free now, may I have the honor of tasting one?

t_ch: 学会新技能不露一手，如锦衣夜行。是时候让「闭嘴」瞧瞧你领悟的技术了。
t_en: To master a new skill and keep it hidden is like wearing fine robes in the dark. It's time for "Shush" to see what you've learned.

t_ch: 对面吧台见！
t_en: See you at the other bar.

t_ch: 去我的主场吧！
t_en: Let's head to my home turf.

t_ch: 吧匙旋转，碰撞杯壁，声响清脆叮铃。
t_en: The spoon spins, tapping the side of the glass with a crisp clink.

t_ch: 大功告成！
t_en: All done!

t_ch: （递上调饮）。
t_en: (Hands over the drink.)

t_ch: （附赠一个冷笑话）。
t_en: (A eye-rolling joke as a bonus.)

t_ch: 提问：为什么舒翁强烈反对把自己的头像印在酒吧的玻璃杯上？
t_en: Question: Why does Siobhan only allow pictures of her face to be printed on their finest liquor glasses?

t_ch: 答案：因为那样她就会变成「杯壁小人」。
t_en: Answer: Because her mugshot isn't a cheap shot.

t_ch: 「闭嘴」尽力保持着顾客的矜持，但你分明看见难掩的欣赏从它的电子义眼中倾泻而出。
t_en: "Shush" tries to keep a guest's composure, but you clearly see admiration spilling from its electronic eye.

t_ch: 这份调饮的味道，竟如此熟悉。亲爱的开拓者，方便让我看一看配方的其余部分吗？
t_en: This flavor, it's surprisingly familiar. Dear Trailblazer, would you mind showing me the rest of the recipe?

t_ch: 唔…它的纸质与列车酒水单完全一致。存在这种可能性，它与最初来到匹诺康尼的无名客有关。
t_en: Hmm... its paper is identical to the Express's drink menu. It might be tied to the Nameless who first visited Penacony.

t_ch: 更大的可能…它出自列车当时的酒保，也就是敝人之手。
t_en: But more likely... it was crafted by the drinksmith aboard the Express at the time, that is, myself.

t_ch: 下次请写完再发表。
t_en: Next time, finish it before you publish it.

t_ch: 难怪这么莫名其妙！
t_en: No wonder it feels so incoherent.

t_ch: 呵呵，不过时过境迁，我已经不是当时的我，这份配方也重重叠叠写满了后来者的见解。这故事简直可以拍一部电影，或许可以将它命名为：「叠中叠」。
t_en: Hehe, the only constant is change. I'm not who I was, and this recipe is now layered with the interpretations of those who followed. This story could be turned into a movie, maybe call it "Mission: Impour-sible."

t_ch: 哦，多么有大作潜质的名字呀！
t_en: Oh, what a masterpiece-worthy name!

t_ch: 看着星穹列车上的又一颗调饮新星冉冉升起，我的眼眶不禁湿润了。
t_en: Watching a new drinksmith star rise aboard the Astral Express, I can't help but feel a little emotional.

t_ch: 我不禁预见到这样一种可能：或许，今后您可以替我担任酒保，而由我来探索群星，踏上开拓之旅？
t_en: I can already imagine it: Maybe you'll take over as drinksmith one day, while I explore the stars and begin my own trailblazing expedition.

t_ch: 而我也会将您的调饮配方，带到脚步踏足的每一片土地。
t_en: And I'll bring your drink recipes with me, to every place I go.

t_ch: 闭嘴——
t_en: Shush—

t_ch: <size=36>想都别想！</size>
t_en: <size=36>Don't even think about it!</size>

t_ch: 你邀请托帕品尝调饮……
t_en: You invite Topaz for a drink...

t_ch: 新抵达的审计团队效率很高，匹诺康尼的资产清算进展不错，总算有了些空闲时间。
t_en: That new audit squad works fast. Penacony's asset clearance is cruising along nicely. Finally, some breathing room.

t_ch: 既然应约来了，就请你替我调上一杯吧。
t_en: Since you called me here... mix me something special?

t_ch: 这杯调饮的作用是，让饮用者<color=#dbc291ff>将自己的所知倾囊相授</color>！
t_en: This drink enables the drinker to <color=#dbc291ff>share all their knowledge when teaching</color>!

t_ch: 唔…工作里憋了太多不能说的内幕消息，不知为什么，喝了调饮之后，真想把它们一股脑说出来。
t_en: Hmm... I've got so many classified insider secrets bottled up from work. For some reason, after having this cocktail, I just wanna spill them all out.

t_ch: 这正是这杯调饮的效果。
t_en: Exactly what this drink does.

t_ch: 好哇，原来在这儿等着我呢。
t_en: Oh, so you were waiting for me here.

t_ch: 有什么可分享的机密吗？
t_en: Got any juicy intel for me?

t_ch: 「以太战线」下版本哪个以太灵比较强势？
t_en: Which Aether Spirit's gonna dominate the next Aetherium Wars patch?

t_ch: 不过，每一次未经审批的信息泄露都可能引发轩然大波，我是一个成熟的职场人，对于所有问题，我的答案都只有一个——「无可奉告」！
t_en: However, every unauthorized information leak could cause a huge uproar. As a seasoned professional, I have only one answer to all questions — "No comment"!

t_ch: 你不知道有多少双眼睛正盯着这个位置。偷偷告诉你，之前叽米先生还私下向战略投资部递交了转岗申请呢。
t_en: You have no idea how many eyes are on this position. Let me tell you a secret: Mr. Owlbert even submitted a transfer application to the Strategic Investment Department in secret.

t_ch: 「石心十一人」！
t_en: Eleven Stonehearts!

t_ch: 都凑够一支足球队了。
t_en: Enough to form a football team.

t_ch: 那么他成功了吗？
t_en: So did he succeed?

t_ch: 很可惜，叽米先生的申请被婉拒了。许多同事不能接受一只鸟有比自己有更高的职级。
t_en: Unfortunately, Mr. Owlbert's application was regrettably rejected. Many colleagues couldn't accept a bird having a higher rank than them.

t_ch: 呃…不对。这种机密，我是不是应该回答「无可奉告」才对？
t_en: Uh... Wait. Shouldn't I answer "No comment" for such confidential matters?

t_ch: 机密和叽米谐音。
t_en: I'm confident you shouldn't.

t_ch: 令人忍俊不禁。
t_en: Heh, get it?

t_ch: 你邀请真理医生品尝调饮……
t_en: You invite Dr. Ratio for a drink...

t_ch: 老师老师，迴星港的迴字，有几种写法？
t_en: Teacher, how many names does the Stargazer Navalia have?

t_ch: 唉…{NICKNAME}，劳烦你替我调上一杯，润润喉咙吧。
t_en: *sigh* ...{NICKNAME}, please mix me a drink to moisten my throat.

t_ch: 这杯调饮的作用是，为饮用者带来<color=#dbc291ff>惊喜一刻</color>！
t_en: This drink helps the drinker <color=#dbc291ff>experience a moment of delight</color>!

t_ch: 一阵急促的来电声响起。
t_en: A sudden, urgent ringtone starts playing.

t_ch: 抱歉，稍等，我接一个电话。
t_en: Apologies, one moment, I need to take this call.

t_ch: 您好，是维里塔斯•拉帝奥先生吗？我很荣幸地通知您，您的学术成果得到了业界同仁的一致认可，被评选为「本琥珀纪最具影响力」的研究！
t_en: Hello, is this Veritas Ratio? I'm honored to inform you that your academic achievements have been unanimously recognized by peers and selected as the "Most Influential Research of This Amber Era"!

t_ch: 实至名归的奖项！
t_en: A well-deserved award!

t_ch: 要小心电信诈骗。
t_en: Be careful of online fraud.

t_ch: 哇哦，教授，恭喜你！是不是会给你发一个大奖杯？
t_en: Wow, Professor, congratulations! Will they give you a big trophy?

t_ch: 呵，虚名浮利，不必在意。是我的哪项研究入选了？
t_en: Hah, just some vain fame and fleeting gains, nothing worth celebrating. Which of my papers was selected?

t_ch: 是《曲空间引擎原理》，《真蛰虫显隐基因遗传学》，还是《忆质能量转换方程》？
t_en: Is it Curved Space Engine Principles, True Sting Larva Dominant-Recessive Gene Genetics, or Memoria Energy Conversion Equations?

t_ch: 都不是，是您那本《石膏塑像的美学原理》，实在太精彩了！
t_en: None of those. It's your book Aesthetic Principles of Gypsum Sculptures, utterly brilliant!

t_ch: 真理医生面无表情地挂断了电话……
t_en: Dr. Ratio expressionlessly hangs up the phone...

t_ch: 这么厉害，我也要赶紧去买一本！
t_en: So impressive! I need to buy a copy immediately!

t_ch: 你邀请黑塔品尝调饮……
t_en: You invite Herta for a drink...

t_ch: 听说你的调饮实验又有新进展了？让人偶来尝尝看吧。
t_en: Heard you cooked up something new? Let the puppet sample it.

t_ch: 能被一位天才频频光顾，可不是每家店都这么幸运哦。
t_en: Not every establishment has the fortune to frequently receive genius, you know.

t_ch: 这杯调饮的作用是，让饮用者做<color=#dbc291ff>最超乎想象的美梦</color>！
t_en: This drink delivers <color=#dbc291ff>dreams beyond imagination</color>!

t_ch: 呼…呼…呼……
t_en: Zzz... Zzz...

t_ch: 美梦？待机状态人偶的后台会如何运行，这个问题我的确还没仔细考虑过。
t_en: Dreams? Never studied how Puppet OS runs during standby...

t_ch: 呼…呼…呼……
t_en: Zzz... Zzz...

t_ch: 黑塔女士，当初课题组决定让你加入，是对你有更高的期待的。可我从你的报告里，却看不见任何创新点。
t_en: Madam Herta, when the research group decided to include you, we had higher expectations. Yet I see no innovation in your report.

t_ch: 能让你参加实验，本来就给了你一个锻炼的机会，你应该感激才对。不要太急功近利，时机到了，论文自然会让你署名。
t_en: Allowing you to join the experiment itself gave you a growth opportunity. You should be grateful. Don't rush for quick success. When the time comes, you'll naturally be listed as co-author.

t_ch: 黑塔女士，给我倒杯水来。怎么，我黑塔人偶说话不管用了是吗？
t_en: Madam Herta, bring me a glass of water. What's going on? Did my authority as a puppet lose effect?

t_ch: 呼…呼…呼……
t_en: Zzz... Zzz...

t_ch: 可以下结论了：人偶不需要做梦，甚至没必要睡眠。
t_en: Conclusion: Puppets don't need dreams, nor even sleep.

t_ch: 你邀请花火品尝调饮……
t_en: You invite Sparkle for a drink...

t_ch: 花火驻场的惊梦酒吧再次开业啦。走过路过，不要错过哟。
t_en: Sparkle's Dreamjolt Hostelry is open again! Don't miss out if you're passing by.

t_ch: 怎么又是这样？
t_en: This again?

t_ch: 您想要重温经典调饮，还是来一杯刚上新的全新配方呢？
t_en: Would you like a classic drink or a newly launched recipe?

t_ch: 这杯调饮的作用是，让饮用者<color=#dbc291ff>成长为潜意识里自己想成为的样子</color>。
t_en: This drink enables the drinker to <color=#dbc291ff>grow into their true essence, as they subconsciously aspire to be</color>.

t_ch: 咦？小灰毛，你这是……
t_en: Huh? Gray Hair, you...

t_ch: 对的，我是花火本火。
t_en: Yes, it is I, Sparkle.

t_ch: 不对，你又使计害我！
t_en: No! You tricked me again!

t_ch: 哈哈，你就承认吧，你心里最想成为的人，就是花火大人我~
t_en: Haha, admit it. The person you most want to become is me, Miss Sparkle~

t_ch: 真有些受宠若惊呀。不过你放心，花火大人会当好榜样的。
t_en: I'm truly flattered. But rest assured, Miss Sparkle will set a good example.

t_ch: 我的心永远只属于星穹列车！
t_en: My heart belongs only to the Astral Express!

t_ch: <size=28>星穹列车天下第一，舒适安全便捷无敌！</size>
t_en: <size=28>Astral Express rules the universe — comfortable, safe, convenient, unmatched!</size>

t_ch: 目光向我看齐，我宣布个事！
t_en: If everyone could please turn their eyes toward me, I have an announcement to make!

t_ch: <size=28>我是花火，我就是最强花火！</size>
t_en: <size=28>I am Sparkle, I'm the strongest Sparkle!</size>

t_ch: 喂，不许顶着我的脸说这种话。给我变回来！
t_en: Hey! Don't say such things wearing my face. Turn back into yourself!

t_ch: 你邀请卡芙卡品尝调饮…但听筒后只传来一连串忙音。
t_en: You invite Kafka to taste the drink... but only hear a series of busy tones.

t_ch: 忙，忙点好啊。
t_en: Busy, busy, guess staying busy isn't so bad.

t_ch: 让星核猎手在百忙中抽空来应约喝杯调饮，的确不大现实。
t_en: Can't expect a Stellaron Hunter to drop everything for drinks.

t_ch: 今后有机会再邀请她吧……
t_en: Next time, maybe...

t_ch: 难道是欠费了？
t_en: Or did I run out of credit?

t_ch: 你给自己充了些话费，但拨通电话后仍只听到熟悉的忙音。
t_en: You top up your own credit, but still hear the familiar busy tone after dialing.

t_ch: 你发现问题可能不出在自己：也可能是对方欠费了。
t_en: You realize the issue might not be yours: Perhaps the recipient has no credit.

t_ch: 希望「花火」不要整出什么「花活」来。
t_en: Hope "Sparkle" won't cause any "sparky" trouble.

t_ch: 该向舒翁提交「特调配方」了，稍后再来调饮吧。
t_en: Time to submit the "Special Mix Recipe" to Siobhan. Drinksmithing can wait for a bit.

t_ch: 还有更重要的事，稍后再来调饮吧。
t_en: There are more important matters to attend to. We'll work on the drinks later.

t_ch: 看你好像很闲，我这儿正好有个「小」实验，你刚好能派上用场。
t_en: You seem to have time on your hands. I happen to have a tiny little experiment where you could be useful.

t_ch: 不会是用我来做实验吧？
t_en: Wait, I'm not going to be your test subject, am I?

t_ch: 我对在空间站看烟花秀没兴趣，你体内那颗星核，还是留着自己用吧。
t_en: I've got no interest in a space station fireworks display. That Stellaron inside you? Keep it for yourself.

t_ch: 求我啊。
t_en: Beg me then~
"Please~~~ Come~~~ Test~~~"

t_ch: 不腻吗？你可以录下来回去听个够。
t_en: Doesn't that get old? Just record it if you want to hear it so badly.

t_ch: 我打算把自己的一部分记忆和知识数据化，上传到空间站的加密服务器，打造一个数字版的自己。
t_en: I'm going to digitize portions of my memory and knowledge into data, upload them to the space station's encrypted server, and create an AI copy of myself.

t_ch: 除了解答那些我懒得回答的无聊问题外，她还可以帮助艾丝妲管理空间站，甚至可能发现一些我都没注意到的有趣研究方向。
t_en: She'll answer the boring questions I'm too busy for, help Asta run the space station, and possibly discover interesting research directions I've missed.

t_ch: 毕竟机器不需要休息，也不会厌倦。当然，她永远比不上我，顶多算是我的一个影子。
t_en: The beauty of machines is they never need breaks or get tired of repetitive tasks. Naturally, she won't compare to the real me, she's merely a shadow of my brilliance.

t_ch: 这个「小黑塔」可不是永久存在的，我给她设了个有效期，等时效过了就把她格式化。毕竟，谁知道哪天这个数据库会被什么蠢货黑客骇进来呢？
t_en: And this Herta comes with an expiration date, after which she'll be completely erased. After all, you never know when some dim-witted hacker might attempt to break into the database.

t_ch: 这不就是AI黑塔？
t_en: So this is like... an AI Herta?

t_ch: 「AI黑塔」、「小黑塔」、「人类智慧结晶体黑塔mini」…你爱怎么叫都行。
t_en: "AI Herta," "Tiny Herta," "Crystallized Human Intelligence: Herta Mini"... call her whatever you like.

t_ch: 那你要我做什么？
t_en: What exactly do you want me to do?

t_ch: 我想找你维护这个数字版的我，看看她能不能应付各种状况。
t_en: I need you to monitor this AI version of myself and see whether she can manage different scenarios.

t_ch: 我和艾丝妲准备了些杂活给你，有些是你需要收集的数据，有些是给数字黑塔的棘手问题，还有则是突发内容…嗯，我得看看她届时会有怎么样的反应。
t_en: Asta and I have lined up several tasks. Some involve data collection from your end, others are tricky problems to stress test AI Herta, and some are unexpected situations... I'm curious to see how she'll respond.

t_ch: 这些委托既是对数字黑塔的训练，也是对整个空间站的小小考验。
t_en: These tasks will train AI Herta while also putting the space station through its paces.

t_ch: 你得搭把手，协助空间站的小家伙们，确保这个实验最终平稳落地。
t_en: I'll need you to pitch in by helping the space station team ensure the experiment runs smoothly.

t_ch: 具体内容已经上传到你的终端了，我可不想在这里浪费时间一一解释。
t_en: I've uploaded the details to your terminal already. I don't want to waste time explaining everything here.

t_ch: 放心交给我吧！
t_en: Consider it done!

t_ch: 你还挺积极的，最近很空？没事干的话可以再测几次我的「模拟宇宙」。
t_en: Look at that eagerness. Been a slow week? If you're that desperate for something to do, you could always run a few more tests in my Simulated Universe.

t_ch: 等下，没奖励吗？
t_en: Wait, don't I get a reward for this?

t_ch: 这种事情问艾丝妲，她会给你准备好的。
t_en: Talk to Asta about rewards. She'll take care of you.

t_ch: 我很好奇数字版的我会怎么评价你的表现。或许她会比我更宽容一些，毕竟她还没见识过太多蠢材。
t_en: I wonder how my AI version will assess your work. She might be more forgiving than I am, since she hasn't been exposed to as many fools yet.

t_ch: 别让我——或者说，别让「我们」失望。
t_en: So don't let me down, or should I say, don't let "us" down.

t_ch: 你还在这儿干什么？委托给你的事做完了吗？
t_en: What are you still doing here? Have you completed your assigned tasks yet?

t_ch: 没事做就去测试下「模拟宇宙」。
t_en: Got nothing to do? Then go run some tests in the Simulated Universe.

t_ch: 我们又见面了，{NICKNAME}，我已经等候多时了。
t_en: We meet again, {NICKNAME}. I've been waiting for you for quite a while.

t_ch: 翡翠，你怎么来了？
t_en: Jade, what are you doing here?

t_ch: 当然是来谈一笔大生意的。
t_en: I'm here to discuss a lucrative partnership, naturally.

t_ch: 来都来了，借我点钱吧。
t_en: Since you're already here, how about lending me some money?

t_ch: 哦？你终于愿意把你家列车长抵押给我了吗？
t_en: Oh? Have you finally decided to put your conductor up as collateral?

t_ch: 直接说明来意吧，我带来了一次新的合作机会，或者说，一次特殊的典押。
t_en: Let's cut to the chase. I've brought you a new business opportunity, or rather a special pawn deal.

t_ch: 只不过在这次典押中，你才是那个提供资助的人。作为抵押物，我愿意以公司战略投资部的名义，向你承诺丰厚的回报。
t_en: Only this time, you're the one providing the funds. And as collateral, I'm willing to offer you generous returns, in the name of the IPC's Strategic Investment Department.

t_ch: 和公司沾边的事都不太好。
t_en: Nothing good ever comes from getting mixed up with the IPC.

t_ch: 没关系，你当然可以不信任公司，但你总可以相信金钱——信用点从不撒谎。
t_en: Not to worry, you don't have to trust the IPC, but you can always trust money. Credits never lie.

t_ch: 终于轮到资本求我的时候了！
t_en: Finally, it's time for the capitalists to come begging to me!

t_ch: 像你这样的高净值人士，向来是每位投资家趋之若鹜的优质合作伙伴。
t_en: High-net-worth individuals like you have always been the ideal partners every investor eagerly pursues.

t_ch: 呵…公司确实拥有遍布寰宇的庞大势力，但我们面对的是一片浩瀚无垠的宇宙市场，总会存在一些隐秘的角落，连资本的无形大手也无法掌控。
t_en: Hmm... while the IPC does have vast influence across the universe, we're dealing with a boundless cosmic market. There will always be hidden corners that even the invisible hand of capital cannot control.

t_ch: 而你作为一名无名客，曾经抵达过几颗危机四伏的星球，并且顺利解决了当地的大麻烦，所以公司觉得你是个不错的人选。
t_en: And as a Nameless, you've landed on several perilous planets and successfully resolved major crises there, which is why the IPC thinks you're a perfect candidate.

t_ch: 当然啦，我个人也对你很感兴趣。你当时在匹诺康尼的表现，可是给我留下了深刻的印象。
t_en: Of course, I'm quite interested in you personally. Your performance in Penacony left quite an impression on me.

t_ch: 呃…我需要考虑一下。
t_en: Uh... I need to think about it.

t_ch: 理解，草率的决定是生意场上的大忌。
t_en: Of course. Hasty decisions are a cardinal sin in business.

t_ch: 说实话，我还是不太信任你。
t_en: To be honest, I still don't really trust you.

t_ch: 没关系，信任关系都是在合作中建构的。
t_en: Don't worry, trust is built through cooperation.

t_ch: 这里不是谈生意的地方，我们移步他处吧。
t_en: This isn't the place to talk shop. Let us adjourn to someplace else.

t_ch: 我们到了，这里你应该不陌生吧？
t_en: We're here. This place should be familiar to you, right?

t_ch: 我说砂金，你上把在搞什么啊？你真有好好计算过损益吗？
t_en: Hey Aventurine, what were you thinking last time? Did you even run the numbers?

t_ch: 哎呀，最后不是盈利了吗？盈利不就好了～
t_en: Come on, didn't we end up making a profit anyway? As long as it's a net profit, it's all good~

t_ch: …我就不信你每次都能这么幸运。
t_en: ...I doubt you'll get this lucky every time.

t_ch: 砂金、小叶琳娜，稍后再开复盘会吧，我们的客人到了。
t_en: Aventurine, little Jelena, the post-mortem can wait. Our guest has arrived.

t_ch: 哎呀，这不是我的老朋友嘛～
t_en: Well, well, if it isn't my old friend~

t_ch: 真是好久不见呀，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Long time no see, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 你们在朝露公馆团建？
t_en: You're having a team-building session at Dewlight Pavilion?

t_ch: 我们只是暂时租用橡木家系的资产，来充当我们的临时办公室。这里设施也完备，交通也方便，非常合适。
t_en: We're just temporarily leasing the Oak Family's assets as our interim office. The facilities here are well-equipped, the location is convenient, and it's perfect for our needs.

t_ch: 况且住在这里的那位朋友，一时半刻也不会回来。这不得抓紧机会，让我享受享受他的生活？
t_en: Besides, that friend who lived here won't be back anytime soon. Why not seize this chance to get a taste of his lifestyle?

t_ch: 我以为要去庇尔波因特。
t_en: I thought we were going to Pier Point.

t_ch: 哈哈，去那里干什么？到处全是一群托帕那样的工作狂，没点生活情趣，连瓶好用的护发素都买不到。
t_en: Haha, what's the point of going there? It's full of workaholics like Topaz, with no sense of fun in life, and you can't even find a decent bottle of hair conditioner!

t_ch: 你趁机挤兑谁呢？我发质可好着呢！咳咳…庇尔波因特人多眼杂，可不是个干大事的地方，还是在咱们自家的地盘比较安心。
t_en: Who are you trying to pick on? My hair is perfectly fine! Ahem... Pier Point's too crowded with prying eyes, not the place for big moves. We're better off sticking to our own turf where we can breathe easy.

t_ch: 既然人到齐了，那我们就直入正题，聊聊合作内容吧。
t_en: Now that everyone's here, let's cut to the chase and talk about our collaboration.

t_ch: 这里正在进行「货币战争」项目的开发，我是项目负责人，托帕和砂金都是项目成员。
t_en: We're developing the Project: Currency Wars here. I'm the project lead, and both Topaz and Aventurine are project members.

t_ch: 战争？！又要打仗了？
t_en: War!? We're going to war again!?

t_ch: 不要害怕，是指商战啦～而且只是商战模拟器罢了。
t_en: Don't worry, I mean business warfare, and it's just a business simulator anyway.

t_ch: 经济我不懂，但打仗我在行。
t_en: I may not know economics, but warfare? That's my jam.

t_ch: 哈哈，这一点我们毫不怀疑。不过在这款商战模拟器里面，恐怕就用不上你那些「朴实无华」的商战手段了。
t_en: Haha, we have no doubt about that. However, in this business simulator, I'm afraid your "unembellished" tactics won't be of much use.

t_ch: 这里的战争指的是宇宙商战，你也知道「万界之癌」席卷了许多星球，给公司制造了不少坏账，清理起来颇有些难度。
t_en: When I say war, I mean corporate warfare across the universe. As you know, the "Cancer of All Worlds" has swept through many planets, creating a lot of bad debt for the IPC, and it's quite a headache to clean up.

t_ch: 我们的对手包括当地垄断财阀、丰饶信徒、反物质军团等等，一个个都不是善茬。他们不仅霸占公司的资产，甚至用武力驱逐公司员工。
t_en: Our opponents include local monopolistic tycoons, disciples of the Abundance, the Antimatter Legion, and more, none of them to be taken lightly. They've not only seized IPC's assets but have even used force to drive out IPC Workers.

t_ch: 在过去，因为缺乏经验和情报，战略投资部的每次行动都需要投入巨量的物力人力。
t_en: In the past, due to lack of experience and intelligence, every operation by the Strategic Investment Department required massive investments in resources and manpower.

t_ch: 而「货币战争」系统则可以模拟各种情况，帮助我们提前为不同形势做好准备。它的本质就是一台巨大的「坏账清算模拟器」。
t_en: The Currency Wars system can simulate various scenarios, helping us prepare for different situations in advance. In essence, it's a massive "Bad Debt Settlement Simulator."

t_ch: 还是换个简单点的说法吧——「企业战争模拟器」，非常好玩，你肯定会喜欢的。
t_en: In simpler terms, it is a "Corporate Warfare Simulator." It's super fun, and you'll definitely love it.

t_ch: 嗯，也可以这么理解。那么，{NICKNAME}，你是否愿意参与这个项目呢？
t_en: Hmm, you could say that. So, {NICKNAME}, would you be willing to take part in this project?

t_ch: 那就合作愉快。
t_en: Then, let's work well together.

t_ch: 来都来了…
t_en: Well, since I'm already here...

t_ch: 好哇！明智的决定～那咱们闲话少说，马上来体验一下初级测试吧。
t_en: Great! Smart choice~ Let's cut to the chase and jump right into the beginner's test.

t_ch: 看到那台正在开机的机器了么，那就是货币战争的操作台。
t_en: See that machine powering up? That's the control console for the Currency Wars.

t_ch: 放心，第一次会从最低难度开始，我们不仅会手把手教你熟悉系统，而且会额外以特殊的形式协助你。
t_en: Don't worry, we'll start with the lowest difficulty level. Not only will we guide you through the system step by step, we'll also provide special assistance along the way.

t_ch: 三位「石心十人」正在热烈地讨论着。粘性价格模型、矢量自动回归模型、逆向选择、实物期权、内生变量、外生变量、流动性陷阱…你听到了很多关键词，但一个也听不懂。
t_en: Three of the Ten Stonehearts are engaged in a heated discussion. Sticky price models, vector autoregressive models, adverse selection, real options, endogenous variables, exogenous variables, liquidity trap... You hear many keywords, but you don't understand a single one.

t_ch: 我果然没看错{NICKNAME}，刚才的表现真不错。
t_en: I knew I wasn't wrong about you, {NICKNAME}. That was quite impressive.

t_ch: 说真的，要不要考虑下直接加入战略投资部，凭你的天赋，很快就能升到高管。
t_en: Seriously, have you considered joining the Strategic Investment Department directly? With your talent, you could reach senior management in no time.

t_ch: 虽然只是道开胃菜，但以第一次参与测试的结果来说，确实堪称优秀。
t_en: Although it was just a warm-up, your results were truly excellent for a first-time participant.

t_ch: 不过，我还是想做一次小型复盘。{NICKNAME}，你觉得在刚才的测试中，击败竞争对手的决定因素是什么？
t_en: However, I'd still like to do a quick debrief. {NICKNAME}, what do you think was the decisive factor in defeating your opponent during the test just now?

t_ch: 你的悉心指导。
t_en: Your careful guidance.

t_ch: 谢谢你的褒奖喽。
t_en: Thanks for your praise.

t_ch: 对手太弱。
t_en: The opponent is too weak.

t_ch: 哦？据我所知，对手可是个商场老手。
t_en: Oh? From what I know, our opponent is quite the business veteran.

t_ch: 我的英明指挥。
t_en: My brilliant leadership.

t_ch: 这确实也算正确答案，自信是优秀投资者的必备品质。
t_en: That's also a correct answer. Self-confidence is an essential quality for any excellent investor.

t_ch: 优秀的团队。
t_en: A great team.

t_ch: 没错。这个答案非常好。
t_en: That's right. That's an excellent answer.

t_ch: 资本市场就像一座华丽的展台，所有参与者不过是展柜里一颗颗等待被关注的宝石。谁能吸引最多的目光，谁自然就能攫取最多的利益。
t_en: The capital market is like a grand showcase in which all its participants are merely gems in the display case, waiting to be noticed. Whoever can draw the most attention will naturally seize the greatest gains.

t_ch: 但最耀眼的从来不是单颗宝石，能把众多宝石镶嵌成一件独一无二的珠宝，才有资格站在展台的最中央，俯视那些黯淡的失败者。
t_en: But the most dazzling has never been a single gem. Only those who can set many gems into a unique piece of jewelry deserve to stand at the center of the showcase, gazing down at those who have faded into obscurity.

t_ch: 所以挑选合适的人，组建完美的团队，是成功的基石。
t_en: So choosing the right people and building the perfect team is the cornerstone of success.

t_ch: 等我拿笔记一下…
t_en: Hold on, let me take some notes...

t_ch: 希望能给到你一点帮助。
t_en: Hope that helps.

t_ch: 没太听懂，还得细品。
t_en: I didn't quite get that. Need to let it sink in.

t_ch: 没关系，在后续的测试中你会明白的。
t_en: Don't worry, you'll understand in the following tests.

t_ch: 我持保留意见。
t_en: I reserve judgment.

t_ch: 看来你更喜欢单打独斗。
t_en: Looks like you prefer to work alone.

t_ch: 最有价值的资产是人——这倒确实是翡翠女士一以贯之的财富哲学。
t_en: People are the most valuable assets. That has definitely been Madam Jade's consistent philosophy on wealth.

t_ch: 是啊。财富、计谋、强运，这些说到底只是工具。想要致胜，最为关键的永远是「人的力量」。
t_en: Indeed. Wealth, schemes, and luck are merely tools in the end. If you want to achieve victory, the most crucial factor will always be the "power of people."

t_ch: 只是一点个人心得，就当是送给合作伙伴的一份小礼物吧。
t_en: Just a bit of personal insight. Consider it a small gift to my partner.

t_ch: 好了{NICKNAME}，你可以先休息下，我们要整理刚才的测试数据，之后还请你继续参加接下来的测试。
t_en: Alright, {NICKNAME}, you can take a break now. We need to organize the test data we just collected. After that, we'll need you to continue with the next round of test.

t_ch: 通过两次测试，你已经基本熟悉了「货币战争」的运行机制，更多细节可以在后续的深入测试中慢慢探索。
t_en: After two tests, you've become familiar with the basic mechanics of Currency Wars. You can explore more details in subsequent in-depth tests.

t_ch: 当然，我也会信守承诺，根据你的测试进度支付对应的报酬。
t_en: Of course, I'll keep my promise and pay you accordingly based on your test progress.

t_ch: 既然你已经可以开始独立工作了，那我和托帕也可以放心离开啦。
t_en: Now that you can work independently, Topaz and I can leave without any worries.

t_ch: 离开？你们不一起了吗？
t_en: Leaving? You're not staying with me anymore?

t_ch: 呜呜呜，你们不要我了！
t_en: Boohoo, you don't want me anymore!

t_ch: 虽然我也很想继续陪你进行模拟，但我们手头的工作实在太多。抱歉抱歉。
t_en: As much as I'd love to continue running simulations with you, we've got way too much work on our hands. We apologize.

t_ch: 我们的数据都存放在「货币战争」里，你可以在需要的时候借用我们的能力。下次见啦～
t_en: Our data is stored in Currency Wars. You can borrow our abilities whenever you need them. See you next time~

t_ch: 托帕和砂金离开了……
t_en: Topaz and Aventurine left...

t_ch: 你不跟他们一起走吗？
t_en: Aren't you going with them?

t_ch: 别担心，我暂时还会继续陪着你。在「货币战争」运行期间，我会一直在这里观察测试数据。如果有任何不懂的问题，你也可以随时找我。
t_en: Don't worry, I'll stick around with you for now. I'll be here monitoring the test data while Currency Wars is running. If you have any questions, feel free to ask me anytime.

t_ch: 别掉以轻心，真正的挑战还在后面。你马上就会见识到资本市场的残酷，那些想要把你手头资产吃干抹净的竞争对手，可是什么手段都用的出来。
t_en: Don't let your guard down. The real challenge is yet to come, and you're about to witness the brutality of the capital market firsthand. Your competitors will stop at nothing to strip you of every asset you own.

t_ch: 测试进度还顺利么，如果累了的话可以先休息一下。
t_en: How's the testing going? Feel free to take a break if you're getting tired.

t_ch: 「货币战争」是谁开发的？
t_en: Who developed the Currency Wars?

t_ch: 如此复杂的项目，调动到公司内外的很多资源。不过，牵头的是我们战略投资部，博识学会承担了主要的研发工作。
t_en: This complex project has mobilized many resources both inside and outside the IPC. However, our Strategic Investment Department is taking the lead, with the Intelligentsia Guild handling the main R&D work.

t_ch: 当然，黑塔空间站也提供了一点技术支持，虽然天才看不上宇宙商业竞争这种小事，但没有理由会拒绝公司的赞助。
t_en: Of course, Herta Space Station also provided some technical support. Although geniuses might not care much about cosmic business competition, there's no reason to turn down IPC's sponsorship.

t_ch: 测试数据是从哪来的？
t_en: Where did the test data come from?

t_ch: 不要小看公司的情报搜集能力，虽然公司暂时还无法进入一些顽固市场，但是当地的市场洞察工作可是从来没有松懈过。
t_en: Don't underestimate IPC's intelligence gathering capabilities. Although the IPC hasn't yet penetrated some stubborn markets, we've never slacked off on our market insights in those regions.

t_ch: 就在我们谈话的此时此刻，无数调查员正潜伏在各个星球的交易市场，将当地的关键商业数据汇聚到「货币战争」。
t_en: As we speak, countless investigators are lurking in trading markets across different planets, gathering local critical business data for the Currency Wars.

t_ch: 不过难免会遇到数据空缺的时候，这时候我们会本着料敌从宽的原则，尽量提高测试难度。
t_en: However, we sometimes encounter missing data, and in such cases, we err on the side of overestimating our opponents, making the tests as challenging as possible.

t_ch: 关于公司的竞争对手…
t_en: About the IPC's competitors...

t_ch: 总有一些自由贸易的漏网之鱼，发展出了独立于公司的商业体系，这种市场体量虽小，但对付起来并不容易。
t_en: There are always some free trade stragglers who develop business systems independent of the IPC. While these markets are small in scale, they're not exactly easy to deal with.

t_ch: 拿我之前处理的兰路思星域来说吧，他们曾长期接受公司投资，但是在一次反物质军团入侵后切断了贸易。等到公司击退军团，他们却拒绝了公司回收资产的要求。
t_en: Take the Lanlusian Segmentum I handled before as an example. They had long relied on IPC investment, but severed trade ties after an Antimatter Legion invasion. When the IPC drove back the Legion, they refused our demand to reclaim assets.

t_ch: 于是我上了点手段，虽然他们顶住了七次大规模倾销，但最后还是在公司舰队的火力瞄准下放弃了抵抗。
t_en: So I pulled some strings. Although they withstood seven massive dumping attempts, they finally gave up resisting when faced with the IPC fleet's aimed firepower.

t_ch: 哦，你别误会，像这种商战进化到热战的情况只是少数，我可是很爱惜「资产」的。
t_en: Oh, don't get me wrong. Cases where business wars evolve into hot wars are rare. I take very good care of my "assets," after all.

t_ch: 暂时没有要问的
t_en: I have no questions right now.

t_ch: 资本市场瞬息万变，你有任何不懂的问题可以随时找我，我很乐意为你解答。
t_en: The capital market changes rapidly. If you have any questions, feel free to come to me anytime. I'll be happy to help you out.

t_ch: 听说你重新开张了？
t_en: Hey, I heard you've started back up again?

t_ch: 嘘——谁告诉你的，小声点！
t_en: Shh — quiet! Who told you that?

t_ch: 为什么又开始鉴定遗物了？
t_en: Why did you start doing appraisals again?

t_ch: 顺应市场需求而已。<color=#dbc291ff>「古代遗迹」</color>里出土了那么多宝贝，我不鉴定还有谁能鉴定呢？
t_en: I'm just meeting the demands of the market. Someone needs to appraise all those treasures being dug up out from the <color=#dbc291ff>ancient ruins</color>. Why not me?

t_ch: 至于搞得这么神秘吗？
t_en: Why so mysterious about everything though?

t_ch: 我巴列维的名声在外，开张这种事可不能公开讲，不然又要有麻烦找上门了。
t_en: Because I, Balaway, have a reputation to protect here. Getting back into this line of business isn't anything you want publicly known, otherwise I'll be up to my neck in it — again.

t_ch: 丑话说在前头，假如你带来的不是<color=#dbc291ff>「古代遗迹」</color>的东西，依旧恕不接待。
t_en: I won't mince words with you: If you bring me something that isn't from the <color=#dbc291ff>ancient ruins</color>, I won't be interested.

t_ch: 「古代遗迹」是怎么回事？
t_en: What's up with the ancient ruins?

t_ch: 你不知道啊？矿工们开采矿脉时，挖出了巨大的地下遗迹。
t_en: Haven't you heard? When the mineral deposits were being prospected, a huge underground ruin was also dug up.

t_ch: 那应该是旧世界留下的建筑，里面埋了不少古代遗物，很多人都去寻宝了。
t_en: It's likely Old World architecture and has plenty of relics buried in it. Loads of relic hunters went there.

t_ch: 你怎么不去寻宝？
t_en: Why haven't you gone down yourself?

t_ch: 我才不去呢，只要别人能找到古代遗物，我就能赚到鉴定费，这不比亲自下场安稳多了？
t_en: That's not for me. I can just have other people bring me relics for a nice appraisal fee. Isn't that much safer than doing it myself?

t_ch: 真的能找到古代遗物吗？
t_en: Are there really ancient relics to be found there?

t_ch: 当然，我这几天见到的宝贝比之前一整年都多！
t_en: Of course. In the past week alone I've seen more treasure than the entire past year!

t_ch: 出于职业道德，我得提醒你一嘴：有人进了遗迹就没出来过，来鉴定的顾客也浑身是伤。
t_en: I'm ethically bound to advise you: Some people who go into those ruins never come back out, and some who bring me relics to appraise are covered head-to-toe in wounds.

t_ch: 你如果还感兴趣的话就去<color=#dbc291ff>大矿区</color>看看吧，要是找到了好东西可以给我看看，鉴定费好商量！
t_en: If you're still keen, head to the <color=#dbc291ff>Great Mine</color> and take a look. And if you find anything worth keeping, bring it over to me. Everything's negotiable!

t_ch: 唉，这可怎么办呢……
t_en: Hmm, now this is a real pickle...

t_ch: 你怎么了？
t_en: What's wrong?

t_ch: 关你什么事…等等，看你这身行头，莫非你就是他们口中的「外来者」？怎么，你也是来寻宝的吗？
t_en: Mind your own business... Hang on — judging by your outfit, you must be one of them outsiders, right? What's up? Have you come looking for treasure, too?

t_ch: 那你可能要失望了，遗迹表层的宝贝已经没剩多少了。
t_en: Then you are gonna be disappointed. There's hardly any relics left anymore on the surface of the ruins.

t_ch: 那帮贪心的菜鸟寻宝者，根本不懂古代遗物的价值，都拿去便宜黑心商贩了。唉，真是浪费！
t_en: Those greedy rookies don't understand the true value of a relic. They just flog them off for cheap on the black market. *sigh* What a waste!

t_ch: 你不是寻宝者吗？
t_en: Aren't you a relic hunter yourself?

t_ch: 当然是，但我和一般的寻宝者不同。对于寻找古代遗物这项工作，我有五年以上的经验，大家都叫我「资深寻宝者」。
t_en: 'Course I am — but I'm not like your average joe. I've got more than five years of experience in relic-hunting. They call me: Mr. Senior Treasure Hunter.

t_ch: 你和其他寻宝者有什么不同吗？
t_en: What makes you so different from the others?

t_ch: 熟能生巧，一项工作干久了自然就有更多心得。
t_en: Practice makes perfect. You'll get the hang of it after putting in lots of hours.

t_ch: 我能用最有效率的方式找到最珍贵的遗物，还能抓住常被别人忽视的寻宝机会。
t_en: I can find and excavate the most precious relics in the most efficient way, and I can grasp opportunities that other relic hunters often overlook.

t_ch: 五年以上就算资深？
t_en: Is five years all it takes to be considered a senior?

t_ch: 当然，这行风险太高了，不是跑到风雪交加的城外就是潜入危机四伏的遗迹，有时候还得想办法把东西卖出下层区……
t_en: Of course. This line of work is too risky. You're always running off to a snowstorm outside Belobog or sneaking into perilous ruins. Sometimes you even have to find ways to sell off a relic out of the Underworld...

t_ch: 面对严寒、机关、怪物的威胁和银鬃铁卫的严格管控，能平安干上个一两年就算了不起了。
t_en: You try going up against freezing cold, booby traps, monsters, and the watertight control of the Silvermane Guards — getting through two years is already an incredible effort.

t_ch: 像我这种有着五年经验的老手，当然够得上「资深寻宝者」的头衔了。
t_en: A veteran like me with five whole years of experience is certainly worthy of the title "Senior Treasure Hunter."

t_ch: 比如现在这个情况，菜鸟们找不到古代遗物就选择放弃了，殊不知遗迹下其实另藏玄机……
t_en: Take this current situation for example. Them rookies will just quit once they can't find any relics, but little do they know that there's a deeper enigma hidden beneath the ruins...

t_ch: 什么玄机？
t_en: What enigma is that?

t_ch: 这…其实不该告诉你的，但听说外来者身手不凡，说不定你能帮忙。
t_en: It's... I shouldn't be telling you, but I heard outsiders have some special skills which might just come in handy.

t_ch: 据我观察，这座遗迹之下还有更大的空间，我能找到通往深处的路。可遗迹里危机重重，听说有几个人已经折在里头了。
t_en: Based on my observations, there should be an even larger space beneath these ruins. I can find the way to get down into the depths, but it's very hazardous. I heard a few people already croaked in there.

t_ch: 唉，虽说这个机会是我发现的，但我终究只是个冒险家，不是士兵…如果能有实力强大的护卫开路，我一定就能找到更多有价值的遗物……
t_en: *Sigh* I may have discovered this opportunity, but I'm just an adventurer, not a soldier... If I had a powerful guard to help escort me, I'd definitely be able to find more valuable relics...

t_ch: 说了这么多，你怎么没反应啊？我的意思是——我们可以合作！
t_en: How can you have no response after all I've said? I mean — we should team up!

t_ch: 你负责探路和保护我，我负责找古代遗物。到时候我把找到的东西卖个高价，我们五五分成，如何？
t_en: You take care of the pathfinding and protection, and I'll be in charge of finding the ancient relics. We'll sell anything later on at top price and split the profits fifty-fifty. How about it?

t_ch: 听起来不错。
t_en: Sounds good to me.

t_ch: 当然，下面的宝贝多着呢，咱俩一起发财！
t_en: Great. There're troves and troves of treasure down there. Let's get rich together!

t_ch: 感觉不太划算。
t_en: Doesn't sound like the best deal to me.

t_ch: 你不会是觉得我的工作比你轻松吧？你知道在黑咕隆咚的地底，想找值钱的古代遗物有多难吗？就算你自个儿找到了好东西，也肯定会被无良买家坑的。
t_en: Do you think my job is easier than yours? Do you realize how difficult it is to find priceless ancient relics in the pitch-dark underground? Even if you did manage to find something good yourself, those unethical buyers would run circles around you.

t_ch: 行了，想清楚就来找我吧，我带你到遗迹里去。
t_en: Alright, come find me once you've had a think, and I'll take you down into the ruins.

t_ch: 时间不等人，咱们现在就出发吧？
t_en: Time waits for nobody. Shall we set off now?

t_ch: 我再想想。
t_en: Let me think about it.

t_ch: 是你啊，准备好去古代遗迹寻宝了吗？
t_en: Ah, it's you again. Ready to go treasure hunting down in the ancient ruins?

t_ch: 进入古代遗迹。
t_en: Enter the ancient ruins.

t_ch: 好嘞，我们出发吧！
t_en: Okay, let's get going!

t_ch: 我想问关于古代遗迹的事。
t_en: I've got questions about the ancient ruins.

t_ch: 问吧，我很乐意为伙伴排忧解难！
t_en: Ask away. I'd be happy to help set you at ease, partner!

t_ch: 这个古代遗迹是怎么来的？
t_en: How did the ancient ruins come about?

t_ch: 都叫古代遗迹了，那当然是古代人造的呗。
t_en: We call it the ancient ruins because some ancient people built them.

t_ch: 你应该也听说过，在寒潮降临之前，天上来的怪物入侵了这里，这应该是那时古人留下的避难所。
t_en: You probably heard about how monsters from the sky invaded this place before the Eternal Freeze. This should be a kind of shelter for people back then.

t_ch: 我们能找到什么古代遗物？
t_en: What ancient relics could we find there?

t_ch: 说不准，古代遗物千奇百怪，很多东西我也叫不出名字。
t_en: I'm not sure. There're lots of varied and strange relics there, and I don't even know what most of these are called.

t_ch: 但不管找到什么，能卖个好价钱就行了，你说是吧？
t_en: But no matter what we find, what's important is that we can sell it for top money — right?

t_ch: 古代遗迹里危险吗？
t_en: Is it dangerous in the ancient ruins?

t_ch: 当然，不危险我还至于找你帮忙吗？
t_en: Sure is. Otherwise, I wouldn't be asking you for help, would I?
